title: Cishet male archetypes: Argentina
intro:
A field guide to the heterosexual male archetypes currently in circulation on the Argentine web and in everyday Argentine life. Sections, and entries within each section, run roughly from most to least common. Glosses describe how each term is **used**, not an endorsement of the stereotype, and many men belong to several categories at once: a Tincho rugbier, a gordo futbolero libertario or a softboy deconstruido de Palermo is more culturally legible than any single label. English translations follow the Spanish terms.

One warning up front. Argentine slang is class-coded to an unusual degree, and Rioplatense Spanish runs on lunfardo, a slang layer where one word (*gato*, *transa*, *gorila*) means three different things depending on context. Terms like *negro*, *grasa*, *villero* or *turro* slide between affection, subcultural description and open classist contempt depending entirely on who is speaking to whom. They are recorded here as features of the discourse, not endorsed as neutral categories. The same mechanism runs in reverse: half the insults below double as terms of endearment among friends.

items.gloss: *(lit. "big-balled")* friend, idiot, discourse marker and punctuation system; the national vocative. Tone does all the work.
*("kid," lunfardo, in print since the 1890s)* the default young Argentine male, whether he is 16 or 38.
*("skinny")* any man being addressed informally, independent of weight.
*("dude," "some guy")* a generic man, usually invoked while recounting something unreasonable he did.
*("fatty")* friend, boyfriend, poster or subject-matter obsessive; often bears no relationship to body size (see the gordo system at the end).
*(same anatomy, bigger)* the boludo you actually mean.
*("boss")* a man receiving provisional respect for completing a basic task.
*("master")* a stranger who possesses information, tools or access.
*("machine")* a capable fellow, usually addressed immediately before a request.
*("king")* a man being congratulated, patronized or sold something.
*("baldy")* a bald man whose baldness has become his entire public identifier.
*("worker," from Italian lavorare)* defines his moral legitimacy through working; the dignified default.
*("sucker" / "rubber fool")* the naive mark, easily played.
*("hung up")* spaced out, forgetful, replies three days later with no awareness that an interruption occurred.
*("WhatsApp elder")* forwards patriotic montages, medical misinformation and eight-minute voice notes.
*("pundit brother-in-law")* immediate certainty about economics, medicine, football, geopolitics and your relationship.
*("serious guy")* claims not to waste time on nonsense; has 47-minute opinions about it.
*("bum," originally a Depression-era hobo)* scruffy, lazy, unbothered.
*("loafer," "scoundrel")* a lovable or unlovable vagabond, decided by tone.
*("goose")* a harmless doofus; also slang for penis, which Argentines consider consistent.

sections.title: Fútbol men
sections.note: The largest single archetype system in the country. Nearly every club label began as an insult and is now worn as a badge.

items.gloss: *("fan")* the base identity; everything else is a modifier.
*(from bosta, "manure")* Boca identity functioning as family, politics, theology and argument-ending credential; a River insult reclaimed with pride.
*("hen," from a 1966 mock chicken thrown at a choking River side)* River identity combining institutional grandeur with an encyclopedic memory of humiliation and revenge.
*("Genoese" / "Millionaire")* the formal nicknames, honoring Boca's Genoese founders and River's 1930s spending.
*("crow" / "burner" / "rat-sticker" / "tripe worker")* San Lorenzo, Huracán, Estudiantes and Gimnasia; the derisive-to-adopted nickname pattern covers the whole league.
*("armchair manager")* diagnoses tactical mistakes after seeing the outcome.
*("football gordo")* has watched your club's reserve team and will invalidate your opinion on that basis.
*(from cábala, the ritual)* repeats clothing, seating, food and bodily movements to preserve a result.
*("jinx")* believed to bring bad luck merely by predicting, attending or existing too confidently.
*("I annul the jinx")* treats acknowledging a favorable possibility as a direct threat to causality.
Messi criticism is not an opinion but a character defect.
regards the post-2021 national team as proof that history ultimately rewards emotional endurance.
*("bitter one")* joyless fan, or joyless man; the accusation of having no aguante.
*("terrace guy")* treats continuous singing as both duty and evidence of moral superiority.
*("seats guy")* pays, sits, evaluates and reserves the right to leave early.
ignores club football, then becomes a constitutional scholar of the national team every four years.
*("sandlot player")* the romanticized self-taught street footballer: gambeta, viveza, Maradona.
sees football as pause, intelligence, personality and resistance to managerial modernity.
sees football as intensity, institutional ambition and technically organized aggression.
results, preparation, cunning and competitive survival over aesthetic innocence. One half of the national football-philosophy schism.
the other half: style, initiative and an elevated theory of what football ought to represent.
*(Boca + River)* resents the gravitational dominance of the big two more than he dislikes either club individually.
*("neighborhood-club absolutist")* regards supporting a nationally successful club without local ancestry as consumer fraud.
understands status through aguante, territory, attendance and capacity for confrontation; adjacent to the actual barra brava, the organized and monetized hooligan apparatus.
produces facial reactions, rankings and transfer certainty at broadcast volume.
*("anti-data guy")* believes statistics are an Anglo plot against anyone who has actually touched a ball.
learned Bilardismo through motivational edits and stories about hidden pins.
*("football-Twitter prosecutor")* maintains screenshots of every incorrect prediction for future prosecution.
*("ball statistician")* xG, progressive passes, field tilt and a dashboard open during the match.
recommends a 19-year-old Uruguayan right-back before knowing whether his own club can afford lunch.
deploys Argentina's anti-luck folklore word against opponents at precisely chosen moments.
believes football culture has been degraded by clips and by people who did not suffer enough.

sections.title: Class, barrio and habitat men
sections.note: The central axis of Argentine male typology: a class-and-taste spectrum, geographically mapped and heavily coded.

items.gloss: *("neighborhood kid")* local loyalty, improvisational competence and a biography organized around several square blocks.
*("aspirational middle class")* experiences each purchase as both upward mobility and possible financial catastrophe.
completes an intermodal transport expedition before his porteño coworkers wake up.
*("posh," also concheto)* affluent, polished or ostentatiously aspirational; actual wealth or merely the performance of it. The leaked 2017 "Cheta de Nordelta" audio is the canonical text.

*("street kid"; turro is old lunfardo for a bad guy)* popular-sector urban youth coding: sportswear, cumbia-derived music, barrio identity. Descriptive from inside, frequently contemptuous from outside.
*(from villa miseria, the shantytown)* a slur from outside, a reclaimed identity inside cumbia villera.
*("greasy" / vesre of negro)* tacky, low-class taste as accusation; the whole word family is classist by design.
*("black of soul")* the ugliest one: class contempt dressed as description, applied regardless of skin tone. Confusingly, negro is also an affectionate byname among friends and family.
*(from the nickname for Martín)* private-school, rugby-and-country-club rich boy; the male counterpart to the Milipili. Went national as a stigma word in 2022.
*("welfare-plan guy," plus the choripán handed out at rallies)* the right's image of the man who lives off the state.
*("rugby player")* club-network masculinity: teammates, social capital, group rituals and an unusually durable adolescent identity; darkened permanently by the Báez Sosa case.
*("has OSDE," the premium health plan)* private-health-plan status as shorthand for upper-middle-class insulation.
*("no OSDE but an iPhone")* capital allocation optimized for visible modernity.
specialty coffee, tasteful tattoos and a recently monetized digital occupation.
*("cardboard collector")* informal recycler; an occupation more than an insult.
pickup truck, polarized sunglasses, development WhatsApp groups and permanent logistical warfare.
*(country: the gated community)* grew up behind gates and describes visiting the city as "going downtown."
*(from Joaquín)* the evolved Tincho: same money, mochilero dress, bucket hat, rolls his own tobacco, plays mysterious.
understated clothes, inherited furniture and inexplicably little visible employment.
*("well-born boy come down in the world")* retains elite codes after the family capital has become mostly anecdotal.
*("little blackhead")* the mid-century slur for working-class internal migrants; historical but not gone.
country-club social confidence redirected toward leverage, trading and Dubai.
*("grill master")* temporarily assumes executive control over fire, meat, timing and everyone else's hunger. Applause is constitutionally mandatory.
*("mate technician")* controls water temperature, yerba geometry and bombilla placement with laboratory seriousness.
*("asador's assistant")* manages bread and charcoal while hoping one day to inherit command.
*("fernet guy")* discusses fernet-to-Coca ratios as though national standards depend on him.
*("territorial pourer")* experiences unauthorized touching of the mate as a breach of protocol.
*("grill constitutionalist")* holds non-negotiable positions on salt, turning frequency and acceptable doneness.
*("the asador who does not delegate")* would rather serve at midnight than allow another man near the grill.
*("price-quality guy")* cannot enjoy an object until he has explained why it was the rational optimum.
knows every discount stack, bank promotion and ghost kitchen.
custom mate, premium bombilla and a thermos carrying system.
*("butcher whisperer")* has a guy who obtains the cut supermarkets supposedly cannot.
*(bodegón: the old-school cheap restaurant)* collects places with giant milanesas, handwritten menus and waiters older than democracy.
*("craft-beer guy")* orders styles rather than beers and mentions bitterness units unprompted.
*("vermouth guy")* rebrands the grandparents' aperitif as cultivated urban leisure.
*("honorary Mendoza wine guy")* detects imaginary mineral notes after visiting two wineries.
*(lit. "condom")* an asshole; the everyday moral verdict.
*("bullshitter," clipped from chantapufi)* makes plausible promises without possessing the means or intention to deliver; a national keyword.
*(from garcar, "to screw someone over")* exploits rules, trust or asymmetry for personal advantage, economic or romantic.
*("puffed up")* thinks he is better than everyone and can prove it by telling you.
*("mask face")* a two-faced hypocrite; also the sober square at the party.
*("thermos head")* supports a position, club, party or president so completely that contrary evidence only raises the temperature.
*(from cancha, the pitch)* effortless superiority; admiring or insufferable depending on dose.
*("smoke seller")* produces confidence, branding and vocabulary in place of results.
*(vesre of batidor, "informer")* killjoy, party-pooper or snitch.
*("snitch")* the rule-loving informer.
*("turd")* a genuinely contemptible man; stronger than forro.
*("pest" / "ball-buster")* won't stop. Was told. Continued.
*("advantage-taker")* treats every informal interaction as an opportunity to gain three centimeters.
*("mandrill")* libertarian-Twitter insult for the opponent who just doesn't get it, popularized by Milei himself.
*("football dad")* experiences his child's youth league as a partially professional competition.
*("the husband who 'helps'")* describes participation in his own household as voluntary assistance.
*("asado uncle")* repeats politically dangerous jokes while managing one technically indispensable household function.
*("absorbed boyfriend")* disappears from the male group and reappears only after conflict or separation.
*("grill dad")* converts family affection into repeated fire-based provision.
*("eternal bachelor")* has preserved adolescent flexibility into an age where it requires active infrastructure.
*("functional divorced dad")* calendar competence, duplicate household objects and highly scheduled romance.
*("café retiree")* holds a regular table, a fixed newspaper and detailed knowledge of neighborhood decline.
*("hardware-store master")* becomes spiritually fulfilled when asked to repair something.
*("child-free dog owner")* parental logistics and vocabulary transferred intact to a medium-sized animal.
*("Peronist grandfather")* interprets the present through a personally remembered political golden age.
*(encargado: the building superintendent)* understands the building, its residents and Argentine society more fully than any owner.
*("divorced at 40, with motorcycle")* reconstructs autonomy through displacement, machinery and new profile photos.
*("Radical grandfather")* retains institutional faith, anti-Peronism and stories about Alfonsín.
*("country-club dad")* weekends consumed by fixtures, carpools and club social structures.
*(from lunfardo chamuyar, "to sweet-talk")* converts verbal confidence into romantic possibility regardless of factual foundation; the national seduction unit.
*(from histeriquear, "to run hot and cold")* alternates attention and withdrawal to preserve desire without permitting resolution.
*("cat")* flashy, opportunistic and visibly oriented toward conquest. Also, separately, a servile lackey, as in "Macri gato"; lunfardo makes you check context.
*(chongo: the casual lover)* remains sexually present and relationally undefined for geological periods.
*("ghoster")* disappears once communication requires discomfort or explanation.
*("almost-something at scale")* produces relationship-level intimacy without relationship-level classification.
*("not looking for anything serious, but we'll see")* wants full optionality and an exemption from misleading anyone.
*("the ex who watches your stories")* maintains low-cost orbital presence through silent surveillance.
*("roundabout guy")* makes simple intentions impossible to identify or execute.
imported intact; conquers, then blocks.

items.terms: Foto con auto / Foto de gimnasio / Foto con perro prestado
items.gloss: *("car photo / gym photo / borrowed-dog photo")* the profile-cliché lineup: passenger-seat access as personality, abs before humor, mammalian social proof.
*("photo with the World Cup")* national sporting joy as permanent dating-market capital.
*("asado action shot")* provision, competence and heterosexual legibility in one frame.
*("wedding photo")* cropped the ex and retained the only professionally taken image.
*("good-morning texter")* generates daily continuity that may or may not lead anywhere.
*("nightclub operator"; levante is the pull)* excels under noise, darkness and compressed decision-making.
*("skirt-ruled")* allegedly overcontrolled by his girlfriend; diagnosed exclusively by male friends losing access to him.
*("tamed")* the internet upgrade of pollerudo, applied to any man who considers his partner's preferences.
imported and heavily used; excessive devotion, zero return.
*("separated with kids")* offers scheduling complexity and unusually concrete expectations.
*("app señor")* older man whose profile specifies that age is just a number.
*("hummingbird" / "womanizer")* the classical pre-app vocabulary for the same behavior.
*("pirate")* pursues multiple women while maintaining strategic ambiguity about all of them.
*(from franelear, "to make out, to paw")* the conspicuously hands-on boyfriend; affection as friction.
*("eligible bachelor, per his mother")* market valuation supported by one highly interested analyst.

sections.title: Political and ideological men
sections.note: Mostly insults applied by opponents rather than identities anyone selects; the grieta names both trenches.

items.gloss: *("lefty" / "little lefty")* the right's catch-all for everyone from revolutionary Marxists to moderately progressive bureaucrats; carries the weight of the 20th century.
*(from the K in kirchnerista, with a deliberate echo of cucaracha)* the anti-K label implying infestation and blind loyalty.
*("gorilla," coined 1955)* actively anti-Peronist, often with a cultural or class dimension beyond policy; hurled by Peronists, worn proudly by some targets.
*("fascist")* the left's mirror catch-all.
organizes politics around markets, anti-statism and war on la casta; the most internet-native political identity in the country.
*("Perón-ish")* reduces the broad varieties of Peronism to one instinctive tribal type.
*("this country is unviable")* experiences Argentina as an ongoing personal betrayal.
*("Argentina always pulls through")* treats the same instability as evidence of national immortality.
*("organic militant")* party identity integrated into work, friendships and the interpretation of events.
institutionalist, managerial center-right identity built around the PRO era.
*("Palermo progressive")* urban progressive preferences portrayed as insulated from material consequences.
*("libertarian gordo")* extremely online libertarian whose ideological life unfolds through clips, memes and long arguments.
*("café political scientist")* explains the electorate through the six people seated nearby.
*("puberty-tarian")* opponents' label depicting the libertarian as adolescent and romantically frustrated.
participates in politics chiefly by circulating images designed to humiliate the other tribe.
*("founding-father account")* anonymous partisan certainty authenticated by the face of San Martín, Rosas or Belgrano.
*("God, Fatherland and Family bio")* makes ideological positioning legible before any post is read.
*("Twitter prosecutor")* treats every trending topic as a case requiring ideological judgment.
*("monthly apocalyptic")* predicts national collapse with high frequency and no loss of status after each missed deadline.
*("professional anti-caste guy")* has converted opposition to political professionals into a political profession.
*("campus Trotskyist")* permanent organizer fluent in factional distinctions invisible to outsiders.
mocking diminutive for the Radical (UCR) loyalist; institutional faith as personality.
*("Peronist of Perón himself")* claims ownership of the authentic movement by excluding nearly every living Peronist.
*("centrist of the rift")* defines himself by being more reasonable than two caricatures he continually amplifies.
*("envelope-taker" / "ad-money guy")* the journalist accused of writing for cash or government advertising.
*("balloon idiot," after PRO's yellow balloons)* libertarian insult for the uncritical defender of Macri-style gradualism.

sections.title: Money, dólar and crypto men
sections.note: Permanent monetary instability has produced the densest informal-finance vocabulary in the hemisphere.

items.gloss: knows the parallel exchange rate to the minute and treats everyone else as dangerously unserious.
*(monotributo: the simplified freelance tax regime)* experiences personal identity through invoices, category limits and fear of reclassification.
*("accountant friend")* unpaid interpreter between ordinary life and Argentine tax reality.
*("Twitter economist")* publishes a complete stabilization plan between breakfast and the opening of markets.
understands inflation as his origin story and every token as his possible escape; after $LIBRA, also as his villain arc.
*("small-business owner")* sees himself as the last productive citizen carrying an entire hostile state.
*("serial entrepreneur")* has founded six ventures and completed approximately one operational process.
*("owner's son")* has a strategic title and unusually flexible accountability.
*("candlestick trader")* reads candles as destiny, especially after the market has moved.
*("course seller")* monetizes the appearance of having already achieved what he teaches.
*("little tree," planted on the sidewalk)* street currency changer chanting "cambio, cambio"; part financial service, part urban landmark.
*("ontological coach")* converts ordinary uncertainty into a paid conversation about limiting beliefs.
*("sales closer")* performs American hustle culture through Argentine voice notes.
*(from cueva, "cave," the informal exchange office)* moves cash, currency and confidence through networks opaque to outsiders.
*(rulo: the arbitrage loop)* small-time exploiter of the gap between official and financial dollars; periodically banned, never extinct.
dance-floor legitimacy grounded in familiarity rather than ironic appreciation.
sportswear, bass-heavy nightlife and Conurbano-born club music.
*("trap artist")* global streetwear and ambition filtered through Argentine slang and local ascent narratives.
Rolling Stones devotion expanded into fringe haircut, Topper sneakers, scarf, gait and worldview; the great Argentine rock tribe.
treats Patricio Rey y sus Redonditos de Ricota as music, community and encoded philosophy.
stadium brotherhood, barrio imagery and distrust of polished cultural production.
*(cuarteto: Córdoba's dance genre)* Córdoba identity compressed into dancing, humor and communal fernet.
*(peña: the folk-music gathering)* regional tradition, guitar competence and an ability to make urban people feel culturally underqualified.
*("eternal metalhead")* still dresses as though an Obras show might begin without warning.

items.terms: Músico que trabaja de otra cosa
items.gloss: *("musician who works in something else")* the statistically dominant form of Argentine musician.
*("rock nacional police")* maintains a canonical ranking and rejects most music made after his own adolescence.
*("plaza freestyler")* believes every social interaction contains a possible battle.
regards the Parque Rivadavia freestyle battles as a founding national epic, which, given Duki, Wos and Trueno, is almost defensible.
black clothing, controlled affect and weekend plans beginning after 2 a.m.
*("after-party guy")* does not attend events so much as continue them.
uses lyrical sensitivity and harmonic sophistication as evidence of superior interiority.
the 2009-2013 tribe that fused cumbia and flogger aesthetics; now a period costume and a word parents still misuse.
owns cables, disappears on weekends and describes unpaid sets as positioning.
band-adjacent, emotionally literate and one acquaintance away from every venue.
*("cultural-center punk")* opposes capitalism while running a cash-only bar.
*(after Ricardo Iorio)* heavy-metal nationalism organized around quotations and personal mythology.
performs unpaid operations management for unreliable adult men.
*("guest goalkeeper")* possesses the highest leverage and lowest social accountability in the group.
protein, hypertrophy and macroeconomic discussion between sets.
*("iron guy"; the gym is los fierros)* attendance as moral discipline.
*("born-again padel player")* returned in the padel boom with a new racket and immediate tactical opinions.
carbon shoes, training app and Sunday occupation of the parks.
*("fishing guy")* seeks tranquility through equipment, travel and very early wakeups.
performs community, suffering and branded functional movement.
*("Costanera cyclist")* aerodynamic clothing and righteous anger toward every other transport mode.
*(from an old brand of fattened ducks)* nightclub-door mass; conflict management as physique.
*("veteran rugbier")* knee damage, club friendships and an unwillingness to admit the third half was the real sport.
buys technical clothing faster than he accumulates wilderness experience.
combines fasting, supplements and ancestral beverages into one optimization protocol.

sections.title: Regional men
sections.note: Circulating stereotypes, not census data. The porteño-interior divide is the oldest one in the country.

items.gloss: *("resourceful Conurbano guy")* navigates distance, infrastructure and institutional friction with practical improvisation.
*("therapized porteño")* highly verbal, psychologically categorized and surprised the rest of the country dislikes Buenos Aires.
a porteño intensified through neighborhood branding, consumption and aesthetic self-awareness.
humor, cuarteto and fernet elevated into an exportable regional persona.
*("Cordoban storyteller")* turns ordinary events into performances through timing, accent and escalating invention.
Central or Newell's affiliation functioning as civic ontology.
regards Buenos Aires attention as both irritating and insufficient.
*(tereré: cold mate)* heat, river culture and the insistence that mate need not be hot.
*(NOA: the Northwest)* folklore, regional history and a firm distinction between the culture and Buenos Aires's representations of it.
*("soy guy")* agricultural prosperity and macroeconomic resentment concentrated in one politically charged crop.
*("Pampas farmer")* production, machinery and distrust of people who discuss agriculture from cafés.
*("wine-and-mountain Mendocino")* wine literacy, dry climate and Andean proximity as quiet superiority.
*("seasonal Mar del Plata guy")* measures time through summer invasion and winter contraction.
*("La Plata indie student")* university, public administration, bands and a city designed on graph paper.
*("Paraná fisherman")* river knowledge, patience and potentially unreliable size estimates.
*("Patagonian oil worker")* high wages, rotational work and weather that makes metropolitan complaints seem unserious.
distance, wind and landscape functioning as anti-porteño moral capital.
*("performative gaucho")* urban man selectively adopting rural clothing, knives, horses or traditionalist aesthetics.
cold-water commitment attached to an unexpectedly durable coastal subculture.
*("anti-map Fuegian")* corrects anyone whose mental image of Argentina ends around Bariloche.
organizes social reality around live reactions, clips and parasocial alliances.
came of age through the first mass Argentine Twitch community and retains its vocabulary.
*("app gambler")* the young sports bettor the betting boom mass-produced; streamers and footballers did the advertising.
*("tweeter")* the high-volume political poster; in libertarian form, the digital militia.
*("virgin")* socially unsuccessful, romantically awkward or simply too visibly invested in the wrong hobby.
accused of repeating pre-programmed opinions; imported, fully naturalized in political Twitter.
*("woman repeller")* possesses an interest, opinion or presentation believed to repel women on contact.
lives inside discourse cycles that most of the country never notices.
*(Taringa!, the foundational Argentine forum)* formed online before social media became respectable; still says lince or maquinola under stress.
*("bait guy")* posts the most inflammatory version of a belief and measures success in quote-post anger.
*("screenshot guy")* archives deleted posts, old opinions and relationship evidence.
*("computer gordo")* computer-native; technical confidence exceeds interest in sunlight.
*("data gordo")* responds to jokes, intuitions and emotions with a chart.
possesses the complete backstory to an incident nobody else remembers.
*(r/Dankgentina)* communicates through layers of national irony, provincial rivalry and deliberately degraded images.
*(Reddit + turro)* Argentine Reddit user combining contrarian rationalism, dollar talk and disbelief at ordinary society.
*("anime account with economic opinions")* global avatar, highly Argentine macroeconomic certainty.
*(carpetazo: the dossier drop)* believes no public dispute is complete until someone's file is released.
designs every conversation around a 20-second extract.
the global archetype localized through football avatars, political arguments and unusual sleep hours.
recycles the provincial meme in which Buenos Aires represents cosmopolitan softness; says more about anti-porteño posting than about porteño demographics.

sections.title: Heritage and byname men
sections.note: The crisol de razas produced heritage nicknames, originally derisive and mostly affectionate now, with the pejorative charge always recoverable.

items.terms: Negro / Gordo / Flaco / Pelado / Chino / Colorado
items.gloss: *(as bynames)* physical-trait nicknames distributed with total indifference to accuracy; the standard operating system of Argentine male friendship.
*(aphesis of napolitano)* Italian or Italian-descended; nearly always warm today.
*("Galician")* any Spaniard regardless of region; also the dim guy of an entire joke genre.
*("Turk")* Syrian-Lebanese descent, via Ottoman passports.
*("Russian")* historically the Jewish Argentine, whose grandparents arrived on Russian Empire passports; today also any blond.
in the Pampa Gringa, the hardworking Northern-Italian farm stock; unlike in the rest of Latin America, rarely means American.
nicknames for Bolivian, Paraguayan and Brazilian neighbors; the first two retain a real pejorative charge.
*("city bus driver")* folk figure with his own humor, philosophy and door-closing justice system.
*("delivery biker")* rider with a sophisticated theory of neighborhoods, weather, demand and which buildings waste his time; the emblematic gig worker.
*("philosopher cabbie")* unsolicited political and metaphysical instruction, meter running.
*("UBA lawyer")* can transform any social disagreement into a procedural argument.
*("gnocchi")* the public employee who appears only around the 29th, when gnocchi are eaten and salaries are paid.
*("dollarized developer")* lives physically in Argentina but financially inside a US SaaS company.
*("IT gordo")* controls the infrastructure, dislikes meetings and may be the only person who knows how anything works.
*(Mercado Libre)* speaks of scale, ownership and on-call load with a mixture of pride and captivity.
international corporate vocabulary attached to salary arbitrage.
says discovery, pain point and north star while ordering a flat white.
*("iron guy" / "nut")* mechanical enthusiasm covering engines, modifications and workshop knowledge; the tuerca understands components, not just prestige.
*(Turismo Carretera)* loyalty organized around brands, family history and national motorsport.
*(plan de ahorro: the car-financing scheme)* has acquired enough financing knowledge to radicalize a dinner table.
*("it's all original")* describes a heavily experienced used car with spiritual rather than evidentiary confidence.
*("farm-pickup guy")* vehicle selected for actual terrain but still discussed as identity.
*(the Amarok, personified)* pickup driver whose truck represents power, impatience and left-lane sovereignty.
*("full tint")* wants privacy, aesthetic menace and limited night visibility.
Volkswagen Bora 1.8T, modifications, acceleration and a reputation inherited from an entire meme ecosystem.
*("highway biker")* motorcycle, leather, distance and service-station fraternity.
*("track guy")* treats public roads as an underfunded racing environment.
*("Falcon guy")* Ford Falcon attachment combining mechanics, nostalgia and complicated historical symbolism.

sections.title: Internet boyfriend zoology, Argentine edition
sections.note: Recurring desirability narratives, not measured preferences.

items.gloss: *("handsome dark-haired guy"; facha means looks, not fascism)* the broad national shorthand for conventional attractiveness.
*("man who solves")* organizes reservations, repairs and transport without turning them into additional labor.
demonstrates competence, provisioning and social usefulness over coals.
objectively unreliable but linguistically difficult to reject.
loyalty, street competence and emotional intensity imagined as romantic assets.
appearance insufficient to explain his success; confidence and conversational rhythm do the work.
*("short king")* overcomes the presumed handicap through charisma, style or shameless confidence.
*("bald with beard")* converts hair loss into a deliberate masculine brand.
*("bonbon")* visibly attractive man, requiring no theory.
affectionate, sociable and enthusiastic, but with fútbol and mate.
*("big baby")* attractive man framed as cute, pampered or knowingly desirable.
older or more overtly masculine version of bombón.
*("hot but kind of a slob")* attractive precisely because presentation remains slightly unoptimized.
*("fanatic but faithful boyfriend")* football derangement tolerated because relational allegiance appears equally absolute.
*("cute rugbier")* high conventional attractiveness accompanied by extensive stereotype-based risk assessment.
horse, countryside, physical confidence and distance from urban ambiguity.
*("psychoanalyzed porteño")* knows why he behaves badly and can explain it without yet behaving differently. Buenos Aires has the world's highest density of psychoanalysts; he is the proof.
*("therapy guy")* treats his therapist's vocabulary as a software update.

items.terms: Pibe que llora con la Selección
items.gloss: *("the guy who cries with the national team")* emotional openness routed through the one nationally sanctioned channel.
*("deconstructed male")* has consciously revised inherited masculine habits and wants this work recognized.
*("self-diagnosed avoidant")* converts relational withdrawal into an attachment-style identity.
*("affective-responsibility litigator")* litigates every message through a comprehensive theory of emotional obligations.
*("performative ally," with the inclusive -e)* uses feminist language primarily as reputational or romantic capital.
tote bag, plants, playlists and a surprising inability to communicate directly.
*("male-manipulator rocker")* sensitive artistic presentation attached to old-fashioned emotional chaos.
*("conscious-masculinity guy")* attends circles, reads theory and is either unusually reflective or intolerably self-monitoring.
treats uninterrupted speaking as both pedagogical technique and democratic right.
*(sobremesa: the long after-dinner table talk)* produces his best theory after the plates should already have been cleared.
*("oppositional reader")* reads the enemy newspaper closely enough to sustain daily contempt.
*("amateur historian of Peronism")* has independently resolved a movement scholars still cannot define.
*(Puan: the UBA humanities building)* theoretical intensity and a complicated relationship with employability jokes.
*("psycho-Bolshevik")* psychoanalysis and left theory fused into a recognizably porteño intellectual type.
distrusts any claim that cannot sustain a three-hour conversation.
labyrinths, mirrors and obscure references as controlled intellectual distance.
curates taste, politics and romantic eligibility through logged films.
*(the national research council)* enters every public dispute carrying disciplinary context and institutional grievance.
regards commercial cinema as evidence that audiences require supervision.
escaped algorithmic media by constructing his own smaller algorithmic media.
*("map guy")* transit routes, municipal boundaries and historical borders as recreational identity.
notices cornices, demolitions and floor plans while everyone else is walking.

sections.title: Street and crime figures
sections.note: As much media archetypes as street ones.

items.gloss: *("thief")* the base lunfardo unit.
*("kid thief")* the young offender as media stock character; cumbia villera made it an identity (Los Pibes Chorros).
*("motorcycle thief")* robbery on two wheels; the modern urban-crime archetype.
*("dealer," from transacción)* the neighborhood drug dealer of villa narratives. Teenagers also use transar for making out; context is everything.
*(from lunfardo malandrín)* small-time street operator; contempt or grudging admiration.
*(vulgar for penis; as a persona, "the big man")* el poronga del barrio, the local boss, admiring or ironic.
*("Reels Stoic")* Marcus Aurelius quotations used to manage breakups, motivation and the inability to answer messages.
*("sigma with mate")* global lone-wolf masculinity equipped with a bombilla and macroeconomic grievances.
*("high-value man")* dating-market language converting personality into a price index.
deletes apps for 36 hours and reports recovering his masculine essence.
global gender pessimism supplemented with local complaints about minas, apps and hypergamy.
uses generative AI to produce businesses, content and a future in which execution is nearly optional.
*("resentful virgin")* romantic frustration hardened into a political or civilizational account.
*(after El Temach, the Mexican masculinity influencer)* imports Spanish-language red-pill content and applies it to Argentine dating.
attempts to solve romantic exclusion through indefinite muscular accumulation.
face, body and grooming as an optimization stack; tongue posture quietly rebuilding his social destiny.
*("seminal-retention mystic")* attributes motivation and metaphysical power to abstinence; the mystic adds energetic vocabulary.
wants a single-income traditional household inside an economy hostile to single incomes.
imagines another country will contain women uncorrupted by whatever frustrates him locally.

sections.title: How new archetypes get made
sections.note: Many Argentine archetypes are generated productively rather than memorized as fixed labels:

items.gloss: gordo fútbol, gordo compu, gordo datos, gordo Twitter. Marks excessive knowledge, investment or online presence.
any belief made impermeable to counterevidence.
gentrified, aestheticized, progressive or consumption-heavy.
ideological overproduction and a weak relationship with offline prevalence.
logistically hardened, barrio-coded, improvisational.
club loyalty, confrontation and an aguante-based status system.
younger, faster-moving, more aesthetic and more dependent on imported vocabulary.
libertarian economics, speculative ambition and possible Dubai imagery.
remote work and partial economic separation from local life.
therapeutic or feminist language without guaranteed behavior change.
upper-middle-class protection and institutional access.
any ordinary type reframed as romantically desirable through practical competence.

items.text: The most Argentine archetypes are compounds rather than isolated species. A gordo fútbol de Twitter, a Tincho cripto de Nordelta, a softboy deconstruido de Palermo or a termo libertario con OSDE communicates class, geography, politics, platform, aesthetics and mating strategy in five words.
